ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general green tea beverage class (FOODON_03301103) but no specific entry for lychee-passionfruit flavoured green tea. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 AN0AN covers tea-based beverages broadly. No specific code exists for fruit-flavoured green tea with lychee and passionfruit syrups. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 34728003 refers to green tea as a substance. The full beverage with fruit syrups is not individually coded; closest parent used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One medium-sized cup of approximately 500 ml, as typically served at KOI Thé and similar Taiwanese tea chains in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Beverages, tea, green, brewed, regular — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 171917); supplemented with estimated fruit syrup contribution based on USDA Branded Food sweetened tea beverage data and standard Taiwanese tea-shop formulation references
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Plain brewed green tea per 100 ml provides negligible energy and macros. KOI Thé medium-size fruit green tea contains added lychee and passionfruit syrups contributing approximately 8–10 g sugar per 100 ml of final beverage. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy plain green tea base plus a conservative syrup-sugar contribution consistent with published Taiwanese tea-shop beverage analyses (approximately 40–50 g total sugar per 500 ml medium cup at standard sweetness). Per-100 ml values are derived accordingly. Sodium is minimal. Fat and protein are effectively zero from the beverage base and fruit syrups.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — fructose-containing sugar syrups and sweetened tea beverages; green tea base GI effectively negligible; overall GI estimated from sugar composition analogous to sweetened fruit tea
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: Plain green tea has a GI of approximately 0–1 (no glycemic carbohydrates). The added lychee and passionfruit syrups contribute sucrose and fructose-glucose mixtures. GI estimated at 52 (low range) based on analogous sweetened fruit-flavoured tea beverages in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables. Glycemic load per 500 ml serving = (52 × 45 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 23.4. Available carbohydrate per serving estimated at 45 g (9 g/100 ml × 500 ml).

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (~10 mg per 500 ml serving)
hypertension.guidance: This beverage is very low in sodium (~10 mg per medium serving) and poses minimal direct concern for hypertension; however, the high added-sugar content may contribute to weight gain and metabolic risk, which are secondary drivers of elevated blood pressure, so frequency of consumption should be moderated.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High added sugar content (~44 g per 500 ml medium serving) and moderate glycemic load (~23)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit or avoid this beverage due to its high added-sugar content (approximately 44 g per medium cup) and moderate glycemic load of approximately 23, which can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation; requesting reduced or zero sweetness at the point of order substantially lowers glycemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High free-sugar content contributing to hypertriglyceridaemia and excess caloric intake
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: While this beverage contains negligible saturated fat and sodium, its high free-sugar content (~44 g per medium serving) may raise triglyceride levels and contribute to excess energy intake, both of which are independent cardiovascular risk factors; individuals with cardiovascular disease should choose unsweetened or low-sugar tea options.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Lychee Passion Fruit Green Tea (Medium) is a popular Taiwanese-style fruit tea beverage sold at KOI Thé and similar specialty tea chains across Singapore. It is prepared by brewing high-quality green tea as a base and blending it with lychee-flavoured syrup and passionfruit syrup, then serving chilled over ice in a medium-sized cup of approximately 500 ml. The resulting drink has a light, floral, and tropical flavour profile: the green tea provides a subtle grassy, slightly astringent base note, while the lychee syrup contributes a sweet, perfumed, rose-like sweetness and the passionfruit syrup adds a bright, tangy tropical acidity. The beverage is dairy-free, vegan, and gluten-free, making it suitable for a wide range of dietary preferences. Nutritionally, the primary concern is its added-sugar content: at standard sweetness, a medium cup contains approximately 40–50 g of added sugar, contributing roughly 180 kcal per serving. The green tea base provides trace amounts of catechins and antioxidants, but these are diluted by the syrup volume. The glycemic load per medium serving is estimated at approximately 23, placing it in the moderate-to-high glycemic load category for a single drink. Clinically, this beverage is not recommended for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or metabolic syndrome unless ordered at reduced sweetness (25% or 0% sugar). It is widely consumed as a casual refreshment and afternoon drink in Singapore's hawker and mall food-court culture, and is representative of the broader Taiwanese hand-shaken tea (手摇茶) trend that has become deeply embedded in Singaporean food culture since the early 2000s.

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry exists in any approved primary source for KOI Thé lychee passion fruit green tea. Values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy plain green tea base combined with a conservative fruit-syrup sugar contribution consistent with published Taiwanese tea-shop beverage analyses. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for sweetened fruit-flavoured tea beverages. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated per 100 ml of final beverage at standard sweetness (100% sugar). Actual values vary significantly with sweetness level ordered (0%, 25%, 50%, 75%, 100%) and ice level. 2. protein_g is naturally zero as this is a fruit-syrup and tea-based beverage containing no protein-contributing ingredients. fat_g is naturally zero by product formulation — no fat-containing ingredients are present. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero as the beverage contains no intact plant fibre. 3. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy green tea entry and is not expected to be present in meaningful quantities in this beverage. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, and selenium_mcg are all -1 as they are not reported in the primary source for this beverage type and are not expected to be nutritionally significant. 4. glycemic_index set to 52 (analogous estimate for sweetened fruit-flavoured green tea); gi_category low per University of Sydney GI Database convention for GI 52. Glycemic load per 500 ml medium serving = 23.4. 5. Serving size of 500 ml used as the standard medium cup size at KOI Thé Singapore. 6. Price SGD 5.80 as recorded from field survey data (KOI Thé — Funan). 7. The description field from the input record (中-荔枝百香绿茶) has been preserved in the Chinese local name and aliases.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Drafted full record. Identified that protein_g and fat_g are zero — added zero-macro justification phrases to notes field to satisfy g4 and zero-macro justification rule.

Iteration 2: Validator feedback indicated q8_aliases_and_multilingual_synonym_coverage failed due to 'aliases_by_language entries missing from flat aliases'. Upon review, the romanized alias 'Lì zhī bǎi xiāng lǜ chá (zhōng bēi)' was present in aliases_by_language but missing from the flat aliases array. This was corrected by adding the missing entry to the flat aliases array.

Iteration 3: Verified aliases_by_language consistency against flat aliases list. Confirmed all strings in aliases_by_language appear verbatim in flat aliases. Verified all -1 sentinel values are documented in notes. Verified glycemic_index is numeric > 0 (52). Verified all ontology match_type values use only allowed strings.
